queries.question: For each geologist, give me the types of specimens that they have
queries.sql: select geologists.name , specimens.type from Geologists_Specimens join Specimens on Geologists_Specimens.specimenType = Specimens.type join Geologists on Geologists_Specimens.geologistId = Geologists.id
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/jobpostings#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/jobpostings/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?name ?type WHERE {
  ?geologists_specimens a :Geologists_Specimens .
  ?specimens a :Specimens .
  ?geologists_specimens p:specimenType ?specimentype_l .
  ?specimens p:type ?type_r .
  ?geologists a :Geologists .
  ?geologists_specimens p:hasGeologists ?geologists .
  ?geologists p:name ?name .
  ?specimens p:type ?type .
  FILTER(STR(?specimentype_l) = STR(?type_r))
}

queries.question: What type of specimens does each geologist have?
queries.sql: SELECT Specimens.type  from Geologists_Specimens join Specimens on Geologists_Specimens.specimenType = Specimens.type join Geologists on Geologists_Specimens.geologistId = Geologists.id   GROUP BY Geologists_Specimens.specimenType HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Geologists_Specimens.geologistId) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM  Geologists)

queries.question: For every geologist, list the type and description of specimens.
queries.sql: select geologists.name , specimens.type , specimens.description from Geologists_Specimens join Specimens on Geologists_Specimens.specimenType = Specimens.type join Geologists on Geologists_Specimens.geologistId = Geologists.id
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/jobpostings#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/jobpostings/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?name ?type ?description WHERE {
  ?geologists_specimens a :Geologists_Specimens .
  ?specimens a :Specimens .
  ?geologists_specimens p:specimenType ?specimentype_l .
  ?specimens p:type ?type_r .
  ?geologists a :Geologists .
  ?geologists_specimens p:hasGeologists ?geologists .
  ?geologists p:name ?name .
  ?specimens p:type ?type .
  ?specimens p:description ?description .
  FILTER(STR(?specimentype_l) = STR(?type_r))
}

queries.question: List the types and descriptions of specimens that every geologist has.
queries.sql: SELECT Specimens.type, Specimens.description  from Geologists_Specimens join Specimens on Geologists_Specimens.specimenType = Specimens.type join Geologists on Geologists_Specimens.geologistId = Geologists.id   GROUP BY Geologists_Specimens.specimenType HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Geologists_Specimens.geologistId) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM  Geologists)

queries.question: What description of specimens is common for geologists?
queries.sql: SELECT Specimens.description  from Geologists_Specimens join Specimens on Geologists_Specimens.specimenType = Specimens.type join Geologists on Geologists_Specimens.geologistId = Geologists.id   GROUP BY Geologists_Specimens.specimenType HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Geologists_Specimens.geologistId) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM  Geologists)

queries.question: For each geologist, what is the corresponding description of specimen?
queries.sql: select geologists.name , specimens.description from Geologists_Specimens join Specimens on Geologists_Specimens.specimenType = Specimens.type join Geologists on Geologists_Specimens.geologistId = Geologists.id
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/jobpostings#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/jobpostings/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?name ?description WHERE {
  ?geologists_specimens a :Geologists_Specimens .
  ?specimens a :Specimens .
  ?geologists_specimens p:specimenType ?specimentype_l .
  ?specimens p:type ?type_r .
  ?geologists a :Geologists .
  ?geologists_specimens p:hasGeologists ?geologists .
  ?geologists p:name ?name .
  ?specimens p:description ?description .
  FILTER(STR(?specimentype_l) = STR(?type_r))
}
